title: Memory Reactivation Levels Remain Unaffected by Anticipated Interference Experiment 2 Dataset
description: This dataset contains behavioral and EEG data from Experiment 2 of a study investigating whether memory reactivation levels are affected by anticipated interference. Participants performed a cued object memory task with varying cognitive load (single vs. dual task conditions), where memory performance was assessed through forced-choice selection of the cued object. The experiment employed a within-subjects design with systematic manipulation of distractor presence to examine memory maintenance mechanisms.
methods_description: Each trial began with a space key press, followed by a fixation dot for 500–650 ms. Then, 2 or 6 lateral objects appeared for 1250 ms. A central cue indicated which object(s) to memorize. The other side had irrelevant objects for visual balance. After a 1000 ms delay, half the blocks showed 4 distractors (dual task), where participants identified a same-category object among them. The other half showed only fixation (single task). A colored dot gave feedback on dual task accuracy. Then, a probe showed 2 objects, and participants selected the cued one using arrow keys. Feedback followed, showing the correct object with colored cues. The preprocessing steps to reach this dataset are explained in the following preprint and the OSF repository [Please provide actual OSF repository URL] (Experiment 2).
